question_text: A light bulb is supplied with 100 J of electrical energy and produces 15 J of light energy. What happens to the remaining energy?
correct_answer: The remaining 85 J of energy is converted to heat. Traditional incandescent light bulbs are inefficient because much of the electrical energy is wasted as unwanted heat rather than useful light.
explanation: This shows how energy is conserved but not all of it becomes useful output. In this case, the bulb wastes 85% of its energy as heat, which is why incandescent bulbs are being replaced with more efficient LEDs.
